name: Torrecilla en Cameros, Church of San Martín, Cueva Ventana, Hermitage of Our Lady of Hope – circular
name_original: Torrecilla en Cameros, Iglesia de San Martín, Cueva Ventana, Ermita de Nª Sª de la Esperanza – circular
region_intro: Jogging around Torrecilla En Cameros offers diverse terrain within the Sierra de Cameros mountains in La Rioja, Spain. The region is characterized by the Iregua River basin, providing river valleys and varied elevation. Extensive forests, including beech and pine, and mountainous backdrops like Mount Serradero, create a natural setting for running. This landscape provides a mix of paths suitable for different running preferences. Best jogging routes around Torrecilla En Cameros The most popular jogging route is Torrecilla en Cameros, Church of San Martín, Cueva Ventana, Hermitage of Our Lady of Hope – circular, a 3.6 miles (5.8 km) trail that takes 0 hours 45 minutes to complete. This moderate route explores local landmarks and natural features. Another top favourite among local runners is Cueva de Jorge loop from Nieva de Cameros, a difficult 8.9 miles (14.4 km) path. This loop offers a challenging run through the natural landscape surrounding Nieva de Cameros. Local runners also love the Running loop from Torrecilla en Cameros, a 5.0 miles (8.0 km) trail leading through varied terrain, often completed in about 1 hour 5 minutes. Jogging around Torrecilla En Cameros is defined by river valleys, mountainous backdrops, and extensive forests. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate paths to more challenging trails. The guide includes highly-rated routes that showcase the best of the region's landscape. More than 200 runners have used komoot to explore Torrecilla En Cameros's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available in Torrecilla En Cameros?
A: There are over 25 dedicated running routes around Torrecilla En Cameros, offering a variety of distances and difficulty levels. These trails are well-suited for exploring the diverse landscapes of the Sierra de Cameros mountains.

Q: Are there any easy jogging routes for beginners in Torrecilla En Cameros?
A: While many routes in the region are challenging, there are a few moderate options suitable for those looking for a less strenuous run. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e990065592/torrecilla-en-cameros-church-of-san-martin-cueva-ventana-hermitage-of-our-lady-of-hope-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Torrecilla en Cameros, Church of San Martín, Cueva Ventana, Hermitage of Our Lady of Hope – circular&lt;/a&gt; route is a moderate 3.6-mile (5.8 km) trail that explores local landmarks.

Q: Can I find circular running routes in the Torrecilla En Cameros area?
A: Yes, many of the running trails around Torrecilla En Cameros are circular, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24550361" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Running loop from Torrecilla en Cameros&lt;/a&gt;, a 5.0-mile (8.0 km) trail that takes you through varied terrain.

Q: What kind of natural features can I expect to see on the running trails?
A: The running trails in Torrecilla En Cameros offer diverse natural beauty. You can expect to jog along the Iregua River, through extensive beech and pine forests, and past areas with walnut trees, boxwood, and hazelnut groves. The mountainous backdrop of the Sierra de Cameros and Mount Serradero provides stunning scenery.

Q: Are there any running routes with good viewpoints?
A: Absolutely. The region is known for its scenic vistas. The viewpoint at Sagrado Corazón de Jesús offers panoramic views of the village and the Iregua River, making it a rewarding destination or a scenic break during your run. You might also encounter highlights like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4579884" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;LasNeveras&lt;/a&gt;, which is a viewpoint.

Q: Are there any challenging trail running options for experienced runners?
A: Yes, Torrecilla En Cameros is an excellent destination for experienced trail runners. Many routes feature significant elevation changes and cover longer distances. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24319007" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cruz de Peña Bajenza – Cerro 1800 Trail loop from Viguera&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 10.3-mile (16.5 km) route with over 800 meters of elevation gain, offering a true test of endurance.

Q: What are some interesting landmarks or attractions I can see while running?
A: Beyond the natural landscapes, you can discover several points of interest. Some routes pass by historical sites like the Roman road along the Iregua River or lead to natural formations such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5322712" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cueva Ventana&lt;/a&gt;. The region also features various mountain peaks like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4067711" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alto de Moncalvillo&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4579875" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Serradero&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other runners say about jogging in Torrecilla En Cameros?
A: Over 200 runners have used komoot to explore the trails around Torrecilla En Cameros. They often praise the diverse terrain, from river valleys to mountainous backdrops, and the extensive forests that provide a refreshing and varied experience. The region offers options for different ability levels, ensuring there's something for everyone.

Q: Are there any routes that pass by caves?
A: Yes, some running routes in the area offer the chance to see interesting cave formations. For example, the popular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e990065592/torrecilla-en-cameros-church-of-san-martin-cueva-ventana-hermitage-of-our-lady-of-hope-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Torrecilla en Cameros, Church of San Martín, Cueva Ventana, Hermitage of Our Lady of Hope – circular&lt;/a&gt; route includes &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5322712" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cueva Ventana&lt;/a&gt;. Other notable caves in the wider region include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3157766" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cuevas de Palomares&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Can I find long-distance running trails in the area?
A: For those seeking longer runs, Torrecilla En Cameros provides several options. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31438213" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cueva de Jorge loop from Nieva de Cameros&lt;/a&gt; is a challenging 8.9-mile (14.4 km) path, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20150338" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Rasillo de Cameros – Olmo de El Rasillo loop from El Rasillo de Cameros&lt;/a&gt; covers nearly 8.7 miles (13.9 km) with significant elevation.

Q: What is the best time of year for jogging in Torrecilla En Cameros?
A: The region's 'perennial green surroundings' suggest that jogging is enjoyable for much of the year. Spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures and vibrant scenery. Summer can be warm, but shaded forest trails provide relief. Winter runs are also possible, especially on lower elevation paths, allowing you to experience the serene beauty of the Sierra de Cameros.
tips:
It began to be built in the year 1545 in a masonry factory. It is a church with a rectangular plan, with a nave with three sections with chapels between buttresses, a transept and an octagonal apse with three levels. It is covered by star-shaped ribbed vaults of different layouts, and has slightly pointed arches. The three-section sacristy is a Baroque construction from the 17th and 18th centuries. The two-bodied tower is from the end of the 16th century.
Inside the parish church, the main altarpiece stands out, built in walnut wood, dating from the beginning of the 18th century.
Curiously, it is just 50 meters from Cueva Mesa and Cueva Silla. Nearby there is a sign that says Cueva Lóbrega.
Small hermitage in the upper part of the town, next to the viewpoint.
